data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T232144-11d55fdc/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T232144-11d55fdc/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T232144-11d55fdc/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T232144-11d55fdc/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T232144-11d55fdc/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-18d83a6a">
    <title>Story 16.3: Specs + cross-cutting e2e + raw-transcript-viewed audit even: part 2</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-70a432b5 is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
FEAT-008, FEAT-010, and FEAT-019 specs are missing the documentation sections required to make the Story-16 run-detail page and its evidence browser official. Without spec updates, the run-detail page, trust boundary statement, and raw-transcript audit event are undocumented platform behaviors.

ROOT CAUSE
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-008-*.md: missing a Run-detail-page subsection describing the trust boundary ('Anyone with project read access sees raw prompts, responses, stderr, tool args') and evidence browser whitelist (allowed extensions + 64KB cap).
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-*.md: Run resolver contract does not include prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement, or normalized ToolCallEntry.
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-*.md: review surface does not point to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- No 'raw transcript viewed' audit event exists in the server or schema.
- Depends on ddx-f04faa86 (16.1 page) and ddx-4cd64068 (16.2 evidence tab).

PROPOSED FIX
- Update FEAT-008: add Run-detail-page subsection with trust-boundary statement + evidence whitelist (*.txt, *.md, manifest.json, prompt.md, result.json, files < 64KB; no Markdown rendering in evidence tab).
- Update FEAT-010: extend Run resolver contract with prompt, response, stderr, toolCalls(first, after), bundleFiles[], bundleFile(path) with bundle-root confinement; document normalized ToolCallEntry as canonical store populated at drain.
- Update FEAT-019: review surface section updated to link to /runs/[runId] deep page.
- Add 'raw transcript viewed' audit event in server (emitted on prompt/response/transcript access).
- Add cross-cutting e2e: full prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence-list end-to-end via Playwright.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend implementation (that is Story 16.1 and 16.2).
- New auth or role-based access control beyond what the trust boundary statement documents.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- FEAT-019 spec updated with link from review surface to /runs/[runId].
- 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted on access and covered by test (TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed).
- Cross-cutting e2e test exercises full prompt visible + response visible + tool trace visible + evidence list visible: test('full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence', ...) Playwright test.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. FEAT-019 spec updated with link from review surface to /runs/[runId].
2. 'raw transcript viewed' audit event emitted on access and covered by test (TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed).
3. Cross-cutting e2e test exercises full prompt visible + response visible + tool trace visible + evidence list visible: test('full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence', ...) Playwright test.
4. cd cli && go test ./... passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:16, ac-quality:needs-refinement, decomposed</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-70a432b5" base-rev="92aaaa58b4aa298f07ec1d225f0736f15b0a5e25" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T232144-11d55fdc"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `b30368f04` (`fix: emit raw transcript audit on run detail [ddx-18d83a6a]`).

What changed:
- Added a `raw_transcript_viewed` audit event on `run(id:)` when `prompt`, `response`, or `stderr` are actually selected in GraphQL. See [`resolver_runs.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-18d83a6a-20260512T232144-11d55fdc/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs.go).
- Added `TestAuditEvent_RawTranscriptViewed` to prove header-only access does not emit the new event, while transcript access does. See [`resolver_runs_test.go`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-18d83a6a-20260512T232144-11d55fdc/cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_runs_test.go).
- Renamed and expanded the Playwright run-detail coverage to `full stack: prompt/response/tool-trace/evidence`, with prompt, response, tool trace, and evidence assertions. See [`runs.spec.ts`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-18d83a6a-20260512T232144-11d55fdc/cli/internal/server/frontend/e2e/runs.spec.ts).
- Updated FEAT-019 to explicitly point the review-surface link at `/runs/[runId]`. See [`FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md`](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-18d83a6a-20260512T232144-11d55fdc/docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-019-agent-evaluation.md).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./...` passed.
